subject: Geology
title: Chemical Sedimentary Rocks
description: A 60-second lesson on Chemical Sedimentary Rocks.

hook.text: When a salty lake dries up, crystals carpet the floor. Chemical sedimentary rocks grow from dissolved minerals.

buildup.text: These rocks form when minerals precipitate from water. Evaporation, temperature changes, or chemistry trigger it.

discovery.text: Limestone can form chemically when calcium carbonate precipitates in warm, shallow seas.

twist.text: Rock salt and gypsum form in evaporating basins. Ancient salt deposits prove that seas once covered dry land.

climax.text: Chemical sedimentary rocks map ancient oceans, lakes, and climates through the minerals they left behind.

punchline.text: Dissolved minerals leave a solid record.

quiz.question: How do chemical sedimentary rocks form?
options: From minerals precipitating out of water
